pr.title: Add non-blocking advisory context-preflight CI check (always exit 0)
pr.description:
## Summary

Wires the existing `context-preflight` into CI as a **non-blocking advisory signpost** on every PR. It surfaces repo-context concerns as GitHub Actions `::warning::` annotations but **never fails the build** — the script always returns exit 0. This is the advisory-preflight item from the loop-enablement plan (greenlit), kept deliberately separate from any hard gate.

```
Advisory Preflight (new workflow, pull_request) → poetry run python tools/ci/advisory_preflight.py
  → collect_advisories(base, PR_BODY) → print ::warning:: lines → return 0   # always
```

Advisories emitted (all judgement prompts, never blockers):
- working-tree warnings carried straight from `context-preflight` (`git_state.warnings`);
- changes to **high-overlap "protected" components** (read from the context-preflight system atlas — `overlap_risk` starting with `high`) without a `Context Basis` section in the PR body;
- new **runner/worker/evidence/attempt** concept modules under `src/` added without a relationship note;
- **provenance wording** (`legacy`, `old-foundation`, `pre-#NNN`, `deprecated`) *added* by the diff without an adjacent evidence link (`#123`, URL, `artifacts/`/`docs/` path, or `BUG-`/`BHV-`/`ADR-` id);
- PR body missing a `Context Basis` section.

Reuses `axiom_core.context_preflight._COMPONENT_FAMILIES` as the source of truth for "protected" components — **no new registry/framework**.

## What changed
- `tools/ci/advisory_preflight.py` (+`tools/ci/__init__.py`) — advisory logic + `main()` that always exits 0.
- `.github/workflows/advisory-preflight.yml` — new `pull_request` job running the script.
- `.github/workflows/python-ci.yml` — added `tests/test_advisory_preflight.py` to the CI pytest list.
- `tests/test_advisory_preflight.py` — 22 tests.

## What behavior changed
- New advisory CI check appears on PRs (green/neutral only). No existing check, runtime path, promotion/evidence semantics, or Revit behavior is altered.

## What did NOT change
- No change to `context-preflight` output or any runtime module. No new evidence framework. 2024 baseline unaffected.

## Tests run
- `pytest tests/test_advisory_preflight.py` → 22 passed.
- `ruff check tools/ci tests/test_advisory_preflight.py` → clean.
- Ran the script locally against `main`: emits annotations and exits 0.
- Tiered testing: CLI/CI-only change → targeted tests only; full suite intentionally skipped (no broad shared infra touched).

## Validation still pending
- None. Not Windows- or Revit-dependent.

## Known risks
- Low. The check is advisory by construction (`main()` returns 0 unconditionally; `run_preflight` wrapped so a scan failure degrades to an advisory, not a failure). Worst case is a noisy or missing annotation, never a blocked PR.

## Factory relevance
Strengthens the verification factory's governance/review layer: makes context/overlap risks visible at review time without adding a hard gate or new framework.

## Revit live validation required?
No. 2024 baseline unaffected.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev
